title: ThermoRoute: a physics-biased, calibrated, safety-guaranteed river water-temperature forecaster
description: Code, configuration, input station panels, and derived artifacts for ThermoRoute — a physics-biased 1/3/7-day river water-temperature forecaster with a dynamic thermal-relaxation prior, a tanh-bounded neural residual (Proposition-1 bounded-degradation safety floor), split-conformal prediction intervals, and a high-temperature exceedance early-warning head. Evaluated under a strict no-future-leakage (Track-H) protocol on 120 USGS stations against persistence, damped persistence, a fair air2stream variant, a global/per-station LightGBM, and a top-down global LSTM, with temporal / unseen-station / ungaged-region (TUURT) transfer tests and matched-seed ablations. Raw observations are redistributed from USGS NWIS (water temperature, discharge), Daymet (air temperature, precipitation), and gridMET (wind) — cited separately in the manuscript.
